instruction: What does strike rate mean in cricket?
output: Strike rate (SR) measures runs scored per 100 balls faced. A SR of 150 means 150 runs per 100 balls. In IPL, elite SRs include AB de Villiers (151.69) and CH Gayle (148.96). Higher is better in T20, but context matters — an opener at 130 for 50 balls builds an innings, while a No.7 at 170 for 10 balls provides a finishing flourish. The league average has risen from 7.94 RPO in 2008 to 9.27 in 2025, raising the bar for what counts as 'good'.

instruction: Why does the Powerplay have both high dot% and high boundary%?
output: Powerplay dot%: 46.59%, boundary%: 19.90%. This apparent contradiction exists because: (1) New-ball bowlers generate swing and seam, creating dot balls when batters defend or miss. (2) Only 2 fielders outside the ring means batters who connect can easily find the boundary. (3) There's little middle ground — deliveries tend to be either dot balls or boundaries, with few singles. Compare Middle overs: dot% 31.97%, boundary% 13.93% — much more rotation, fewer extremes. The Powerplay is the most volatile phase in T20 cricket.

instruction: What is a good economy rate for an IPL bowler?
